executed_runs.notes: Pitch runtime and Docker preflight gate for all real-runtime evidence.

executed_runs.label: surface audit
executed_runs.notes: Confirms the bundled jars expose a live vendor-specific 202X surface without overclaiming IEEE 2025 support.

executed_runs.label: smoke
executed_runs.notes: Exercises the smallest promoted Pitch lifecycle and exchange smoke on the vendor runtime.

executed_runs.label: time window future exclusion
executed_runs.notes: Trial-safe two-federate Target/Radar future-exclusion proof for vendor credence.

executed_runs.label: time window restore state

known_boundaries.summary: The checked-in pitch-202x backend routes still wrap the repo Python 2025 backend and do not count as real vendor-runtime evidence.
Negotiated ownership remains a documented bridge-divergent branch and is intentionally excluded from the 202x-certify promoted claim.
The restore-output Target/Radar proof needs three federates and is outside the current trial-safe Pitch route set.
Save/restore, DDM, and lost-federate remain explicit Pitch gap/probe families and are not part of this 202X certification packet.

scope: Pitch vendor 202X surface plus trial-safe real-runtime vendor-credence packet; this is not an IEEE 1516-2025 conformance claim.

blocker_findings.reason: Common conversion and callback dispatch code imports 2010 enums, exceptions, handles, time classes, and API metadata directly.
Generic Java RTI discovery is still implemented as a 2010-only factory probe.

time_window_vendor_parity_audit.current_assessment: The Target/Radar time-window ladder now has an explicit vendor-parity shape audit. The future-exclusion route is intentionally two-federate-safe and is the right Pitch trial candidate; the current Pitch gap is runtime seat availability, not an overgrown scenario topology.

current_trial_candidate.why_selected: This is the smallest lookahead-window proof that still exercises future-message exclusion. It keeps the topology to two federates, so a successful Pitch run would add meaningful vendor credence without depending on the larger multi-federate gauntlet.

time_window_vendor_parity_audit.residual_boundary: A green Pitch result would add vendor credence for the two-federate future-exclusion proof, but it would still not replace the broader in-process and hosted Python evidence for output delivery, consumer order, pipeline overlap, or save/restore window replay.

routes.current_pitch_runtime_boundary_evidence: Pitch managed runtime currently reports no available pRTI federate seats for this two-federate future-exclusion proof.
routes.purpose: Prove radar window closure stays blocked while another regulating federate can still legally send into the closing window, then prove closure becomes legal only after that future input is excluded.

routes.current_pitch_runtime_boundary_evidence: Pitch managed runtime currently reports no available pRTI federate seats for this two-federate time-window restore proof.
routes.purpose: Prove that restore reopens or recloses the radar window at the saved point without leaking dirty future state across the checkpoint boundary.

routes.current_pitch_runtime_boundary_evidence: Pitch managed runtime currently reports no available pRTI federate seats for this three-federate time-window restore-output proof.
routes.purpose: Prove restore resumes legal post-window output delivery without duplicate replay to the consumer.

trial_safe_scenario_allowlist.notes: Basic example FOM lifecycle plus object/interaction exchange under the promoted smoke lane.
Prove radar window closure stays blocked while another regulating federate can still legally send into the closing window, then prove closure becomes legal only after that future input is excluded.
Prove that restore reopens or recloses the radar window at the saved point without leaking dirty future state across the checkpoint boundary.
